narrative: Your law school application was denied because our predictive model, which assesses the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try, estimated a 48.2% chance that you would not pass—above the threshold we use for admission decisions. This prediction draws from your academic record, including your undergraduate GPA of 2.80, LSAT score of 32, and class decile positions of 3rd in both your first and third years, alongside details like your upper-middle family income and plans to attend full-time. Among students who passed the bar, the average undergraduate GPA is 3.24 and LSAT is 37.27, with first-year deciles averaging 6th and third-year around 6th, showing your metrics fell notably below those benchmarks. The three biggest factors shaping this prediction were your undergraduate GPA, third-year class decile, and LSAT score. Your undergraduate GPA stood out as the strongest positive influence, suggesting it helped pull the prediction toward expecting you to pass despite other concerns. In contrast, your third-year decile ranking pushed notably toward the failure prediction, as being in the 3rd decile signals performance that the model links to bar struggles. Similarly, your LSAT score contributed toward failure by falling short of levels typically seen in passers. Your upper-middle family income aligns well with many who succeed, and full-time status is common among them too, but these didn't outweigh the key academic signals. Compared to admitted students, whose profiles cluster around stronger GPAs, higher LSATs, and top-half deciles, your combination tipped the scales against approval. This isn't a judgment on your potential or background—it's a data-driven forecast to help the school select those with the highest predicted bar success. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score, strengthening your undergrad record through additional coursework or a post-bac program, and reapplying with updated decile improvements from further studies. Many students in similar positions have succeeded by targeting these areas, and we're here to support your next steps.
